title: 10 best herbal decoctions for numbness in hands
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for numbness in hands are a natural remedy that combines herbs, roots, and botanicals to soothe and relieve tingling sensations in the fingers. These concoctions work by increasing blood flow, reducing inflammation, and calming nerve endings, thereby alleviating numbness and discomfort. For instance, decoctions made with ginseng, turmeric, and ginger have been shown to effectively reduce numbness in hands, allowing individuals to regain sensitivity and mobility. This improved circulation and reduced numbness enables people to perform daily tasks with ease, enhancing their overall quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree decoctions helps with numbness in hands because it contains a unique combination of flavonoids, polyphenols, and other bioactive compounds that help to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation. The antioxidants present in the decoction also help to neutralize free radicals that can cause numbness and tingling sensations. By increasing oxygen and nutrient supply to the nerves, maidenhair tree decoctions may help to alleviate symptoms of peripheral neuropathy, allowing for improved sensation and function in the hands.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant compounds present in Maidenhair Tree decoctions help to neutralize free radicals that can damage nerve cells and contribute to numbness and tingling sensations.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Maidenhair Tree decoctions reduce inflammation in the affected areas, which can help alleviate numbness caused by conditions such as peripheral neuropathy or carpal tunnel syndrome.

Nerve-protective flavonoids: The flavonoid compounds found in Maidenhair Tree decoctions have been shown to protect nerve cells from damage and promote the regeneration of damaged nerves, helping to restore sensation and alleviate numbness.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Ginkgo biloba help improve blood flow to the hands by enhancing the production of nitric oxide, which relaxes blood vessels and increases oxygen supply to numb hands.

Terpenoids (Ginkgolides and Bilobalide): Terpenoids in Ginkgo biloba have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the hands, which may contribute to numbness.

Bilobalide: Bilobalide, a terpenoid in Ginkgo biloba, has neuroprotective properties that help improve blood flow to the hands by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in nerve cells, which may alleviate numbness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary source of ginkgo biloba's active compounds, including flavonoids and terpenoids, which help improve blood circulation and alleviate numbness.
Barks: The bark of the ginkgo tree contains flavonoids and terpenoids, which may help to improve circulation and reduce numbness in hands.
Seeds: Ginkgo seeds are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which can help to improve blood circulation and reduce numbness in the hands.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 4-8 ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees in the fall or spring seasons.
Dry the leaves in a warm place for 1-2 weeks to reduce moisture content.
Crush the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine 2-3 grams of the powdered leaves with 1 liter of water in a saucepan.
Simmer the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes to extract active compounds.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric decoctions helps with numbness in hands because it contains a powerful compound called curcumin, which has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. When consumed as a decoction, curcumin is absorbed into the bloodstream and transported to areas of inflammation, such as joints and extremities like the hands. There, it reduces swelling, soothes irritation, and promotes healing, thereby alleviating numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Turmeric's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as curcumin, reduce swelling and inflammation in the hands, which can help alleviate numbness caused by conditions like carpal tunnel syndrome or arthritis.
Antioxidant property: Turmeric's antioxidants combat free radicals that can damage nerve cells and contribute to numbness, promoting overall nerve health and function.

Neuroprotective property: Turmeric's neuroprotective compounds, such as curcuminoids, have been shown to protect against nerve damage and degeneration, potentially helping to alleviate numbness caused by conditions like peripheral neuropathy.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin, a phenolic compound, helps with numbness in hands by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to numbness and tingling sensations.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC, a curcuminoid, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate numbness in hands by preventing the damage of nerve cells and reducing pain.

Beta-Caryophyllene: Beta-Caryophyllene, a sesquiterpene, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that help with numbness in hands by reducing inflammation, promoting nerve regeneration, and relieving pain.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Used for their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to relieve numbness and pain in the hands.
Roots: Utilized for their warming and stimulating effects, which can increase blood circulation and reduce numbness in the hands.
Leaves: Employed for their ability to promote blood flow and relieve numbness, making them a popular ingredient in traditional remedies.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 to 3 grams of dried curcuma longa rhizomes and clean them thoroughly under cold running water.
Boil 200 milliliters of water and add the dried curcuma longa rhizomes to the boiling water.
Reduce heat to a simmer and let the mixture steep for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup.
Allow the decoction to cool down before consuming it as a herbal remedy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger decoctions helps with numbness in hands because they improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to relax the blood vessels and increase blood flow to the extremities. This increased blood flow helps to alleviate numbness and tingling sensations in the hands by providing oxygen and nutrients to the affected areas. Additionally, ginger's warming and relaxing effects can also help to ease muscle tension and discomfort associated with numbness.
remedy_properties: Analgesic properties: Ginger's analgesic properties help to relieve pain and discomfort associated with numbness in hands, by reducing inflammation and soothing irritated nerves.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the hands and wrists, which can contribute to numbness and tingling sensations.

Vasodilatory properties: Ginger's vasodilatory effects help to increase blood flow and circulation to the hands and wrists, which can help to alleviate numbness and tingling sensations by providing more oxygen and nutrients to the affected areas.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols, particularly 6-gingerol and 8-gingerol, possess anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help reduce pain and numbness in hands by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and mediators.

Shogaols: Shogaols, a type of gingerol derivative, exhibit potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities that help alleviate numbness in hands by scavenging free radicals, reducing inflammation, and modulating the nervous system.

Zingiberene: Zingiberene, a sesquiterpene, has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help relieve numbness in hands by inhibiting the activity of pro-inflammatory enzymes and mediators, and promoting the release of pain-relieving neurotransmitters.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The most used part, as they contain high concentrations of gingerols and shogaols, compounds that provide pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Also commonly used, as they contain similar compounds to rhyzomes and are often harvested and dried for later use in decoctions.
Stems: Although less commonly mentioned, some sources suggest that ginger stems can be used to make decoctions for numbness in hands, potentially due to their secondary content of ginger compounds.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 grams of dried zingiber officinale root and store it in a clean dry container.
Combine 250 milliliters of boiling water with the dried zingiber officinale root in a saucepan.
Allow the zingiber officinale root to steep in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a separate container.
Store the herbal decoction in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper decoctions helps with numbness in hands because of its ability to increase blood flow and reduce inflammation. The antioxidants present in bell peppers, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, help to protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to numbness and tingling sensations. remedy_properties: Capsaicin-rich decoctions: Capsicum annuum (cayenne pepper or chili peppers) contain capsaicin, which can help increase blood flow and reduce numbness by stimulating the release of neurotransmitters that help regulate pain.

Ginkgo biloba extract: Ginkgo biloba is known for its ability to improve circulation and reduce numbness in extremities due to its flavonoids, which dilate blood vessels and promote healthy blood flow.

Turmeric decoctions: Curcumin, a compound found in turmeric (Curcuma longa), has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate numbness in hands by reducing inflammation and improving circulation.
remedy_constituents: Capsaicin: Capsaicin helps with numbness in hands by blocking the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain, thereby reducing inflammation and relieving pain in the hands.

Beta-Carotene: Beta-Carotene helps with numbness in hands by acting as an antioxidant that reduces oxidative stress and promotes the healing of damaged nerve endings, thus alleviating numbness and pain in the hands.

Quercetin: Quercetin helps with numbness in hands by inhibiting the production of inflammatory chemicals, such as prostaglandins and leukotrienes, which contribute to pain and inflammation in the hands, thereby providing relief from numbness.
remedy_parts: Fruits: The fruits of Capsicum annuum, specifically the ripe ones, are commonly used to make decoctions for numbness in hands due to their high capsaicin content, which helps to stimulate blood flow and relieve numbness.
Leaves: The leaves of Capsicum annuum are used in decoctions for numbness in hands because they contain capsaicin, which helps to increase circulation and reduce numbness.
Seeds: The seeds of Capsicum annuum are used in decoctions for numbness in hands due to their high concentration of capsaicin, which helps to stimulate blood flow and relieve numbness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 grams of fresh capsicum annuum fruits from your garden or a trusted supplier.
Cut the fruits into small pieces and combine them with 2 liters of water in a saucepan.
Boil the mixture over high heat for 15-20 minutes or until the liquid has reduced by half.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days or freeze it for later use.

remedy_recipe: Gather 2-4 grams of dried withania somnifera root and 1 cup of water for decoction.
Weigh 2-4 grams of dried withania somnifera root and place it in a saucepan.
Boil 1 cup of water for 5-10 minutes and then add the withania somnifera root.
Simmer the mixture for 10-15 minutes or until the liquid is reduced to half a cup.
Strain the decoction and discard the solids to obtain a final herbal remedy.

remedy_recipe: Gather 1-2 teaspoons of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and 2 cups of water for decoction.
Combine the roots and water in a pot and bring to a boil at 212 degrees fahrenheit.
Reduce heat to a simmer for 5-10 minutes or until the liquid has reduced slightly.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a separate container.
Allow the decoction to cool before refrigerating and consuming within 24 hours.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh or 1/4 cup dried mentha x piperita leaves and flowers.
Chop the harvested leaves and flowers into small pieces for better infusion.
Combine the chopped mentha x piperita with 2 cups of boiling water in a saucepan.
Reduce heat and simmer the mixture for 5-10 minutes to release the active compounds.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 to 30 grams of fresh or 10 to 20 grams of dried plant material at the peak of flowering.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh bark of populus balsamifera, drying it completely within three days to ensure potency preservation.
Grind dried populus balsamifera bark into fine powder using a mortar and pestle, obtaining 20 grams of the mixture.
Combine ground populus balsamifera bark with 400 milliliters of distilled water in a heat-resistant glass container, allowing the mixture to steep for 30 minutes at room temperature.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another container, discarding solids and reserving the clear decoction for further use.
Bring reserved decoction to a boil and reduce heat to simmer for an additional 15-minute period before serving it warm as needed to alleviate numbness in hands.

remedy_recipe: Measure 2 tablespoons of dried thymus vulgaris herb and add it to a heat-resistant container.
Pour 250 milliliters of boiling water over the herb and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another container discarding the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool down to room temperature before refrigerating it for up to 24 hours.
Take 1/2 cup of the cooled decoction 2-3 times daily as needed and monitor numbness in hands.

remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried avena sativa herb into a clean glass cup for each liter of water needed.
Combine the measured avena sativa herb and water in a saucepan over low heat, stirring occasionally.
Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer and maintain it at 180-190°f (82-88°c) for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another clean glass cup, discarding the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature before transferring it to an amber glass bottle with tight-fitting lid, storing it in a dark place for up to three days.

remedy_recipe: Measure one tablespoon of dried taraxacum officinale root powder into a small bowl.
Heat one cup of water in a pot until it reaches boiling point and then reduce heat.
Add the measured taraxacum officinale root powder to the hot water and steep for 15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another container and discard solids.

remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried arctium lappa roots and 4 cups of water.
Combine the ingredients in a medium-sized pot and bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes allowing the flavors to meld together.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality tanacetum parthenium flowers and leaves according to local regulations and guidelines.
Rinse the collected plant material gently with filtered water to remove dirt debris and contaminants.
Weigh out 20-30 grams of fresh tanacetum parthenium plant material or 10-15 grams of dried material for each decoction batch.
Combine the weighed plant material with 1 liter of boiling distilled water in a heat-resistant glass container and allow it to steep for 30 minutes to 1 hour.
Strain the decoction using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another clean container discarding the solids and storing the liquid in the refrigerator at 4-8°c for up to 3 days.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh humulus lupulus leaves and flowers or 1 ounce of dried herb and rinse thoroughly.
Combine the harvested herb with 4 cups of purified water in a medium-sized saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 20-30 minutes or until the liquid has reduced by half and the flavor is potent.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a large glass container and discard solids.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature and store in the refrigerator for up to 3 days for use as needed.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to numbness in hands that are treated with herbal decoctions are conditions like arthritis, carpal tunnel syndrome, and nerve damage. Herbal decoctions containing turmeric, ginger, and ginseng have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate pain and numbness in the hands. Decoctions made from St. John's Wort, ashwagandha, and devil's claw can also provide relief from joint pain and stiffness associated with these conditions.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal decoctions that help with numbness in hands is a blend of Ginger, Turmeric, and Ashwagandha. Ginger's warming properties and anti-inflammatory compounds help to increase blood flow and reduce pain, while Turmeric's curcumin content reduces inflammation and promotes healing. Ashwagandha, an adaptogenic herb, helps to balance the body's response to stress, which can contribute to numbness in the hands. Combine 1 part of each decoction and drink twice a day to alleviate numbness and improve hand circulation.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Complementary therapies in clinical practice, herbal decoctions have been found to be beneficial for managing numbness in hands associated with diabetic peripheral neuropathy (DPN). The study, which included 72 randomized controlled trials with a total of 6260 patients, found that herbal medicine and co-administration of herbal medicine with methylcobalamin significantly increased the sensory and motor nerve conduction velocities of the median and common peroneal nerves compared to methylcobalamin treatment alone. This improvement in nerve conduction velocities suggests that herbal decoctions may help alleviate numbness in hands by promoting nerve health and function.
